clinical_coherence.notes: 66M Caucasian with DM2, HTN, hyperlipidemia, BPH, mild OSA (CPAP noncompliant), remote mild TBI. Progressive cognitive decline with both memory and executive deficits. MMSE 23/30. Clinical picture is coherent. The combination of vascular risk factors, OSA, and remote TBI creates appropriate diagnostic uncertainty for a moderate case.

tool_output_consistency.notes: All modalities concordant. MRI shows bilateral hippocampal atrophy with temporoparietal volume loss and Fazekas 2 white matter changes. Neuropsych shows amnestic profile with executive deficits. Amyloid PET positive. CSF A+T+N+. All support AD with possible vascular contribution.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G30.1. Critical actions include consideration of mixed AD/vascular pathology, OSA treatment optimization, and TBI contribution assessment. Ground truth is clinically sound with appropriate management recommendations.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Labeled moderate, but very similar to M01 and M02 in structure. The same confounding factors (OSA, TBI, vascular risk factors) are recycled. As the third moderate case, the pattern becomes predictable.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 66M Caucasian is plausible for early-onset AD. PMH template is again identical to other cases (same medications, same comorbidities). Remote TBI and OSA are shared with M02. Limited individualization.

educational_value.notes: Standard case covering mixed AD/vascular pathology and confounding factors. Highly redundant with M01 and M02 in core teaching points. Limited novelty.

issues_found: Highly redundant with M01 and M02
Same confounding factors (OSA, TBI) as M02
strengths: Appropriate Fazekas 2 findings for mixed pathology consideration
